docs.title: Blog
docs.text:
Blog 用来发布有明确上下文、判断过程和维护价值的文章。它不是命令摘抄区，也不是临时变更日志；每篇文章都应该帮助读者理解一个真实的 DevOps 工程问题。

Architecture AWS EKS 生产级高可用集群 以约束、失败模式、交付阶段和回滚策略拆解生产级 EKS 高可用设计。 Deep Dives 深度文章入口 从高可用、回滚、告警、成本、平台化和安全治理建立反直觉判断。 Decision Reviews 错误决策复盘 用走过的弯路证明判断力，而不是只展示成功结果。

docs.title: 发布标准
docs.text:
一篇文章进入 Blog 前，至少要满足四个条件：

标准 要求 有问题背景 读者能知道这篇文章解决什么生产问题，而不是只看到工具名 有约束边界 说明适用场景、不适用场景、组织或成本约束 有判断证据 给出失败模式、指标、事故症状、迁移阶段或取舍理由 有可复用结论 读者能把结论用于自己的系统评估、方案设计或复盘

docs.title: 订阅与检索
docs.text: RSS 订阅中文文章更新。 Tags 按 Architecture、Reliability、Delivery、Platform 等主题检索。 Decision Reviews 从错误决策、约束变化和取舍证据中提炼可复用判断。

docs.title: 后续文章来源
docs.text: 后续文章优先从已有正式栏目中产生：Deep Dives、Case Studies、Decision Reviews、Incident Reviews 和 Production Architecture。只有当文章具备真实约束、失败模式和可验证结论时，才会进入 Blog。

docs.title: Case Type
docs.text: Traditional service to container platform migration.

docs.title: Background
docs.text: A legacy application stack needed to move from manually managed hosts to a containerized deployment model. The original diagrams and service names are not published.

docs.title: Standard Migration Model
docs.text:
1. Inventory services, ports, dependencies, data paths, and startup order.
  2. Build container images with pinned base images.
  3. Externalize configuration.
  4. Move credentials into secret management.
  5. Define health checks.
  6. Deploy to staging.
  7. Run smoke tests.
  8. Cut over traffic gradually.
  9. Keep rollback path to the original deployment until stable.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
• Container starts reliably.
  • Logs are available.
  • Health endpoint passes.
  • Database and cache connections work.
  • Ingress or gateway route works.
  • Monitoring and alerts cover the new deployment.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• Docker Compose Stack Standard
  • Kubernetes Cluster Provisioning Standard

docs.title: Case Type
docs.text: Kubernetes platform upgrade and workload migration.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
An organization operated a Kubernetes cluster running an older minor version. The cluster hosted multiple stateless services, ingress routes, monitoring components, and stateful dependencies. The platform needed to move to a newer supported version while minimizing service interruption.

All identifiers in this case are generic. No original service names, domains, regions, accounts, repositories, or customer details are preserved.

docs.title: Challenges
docs.text:
• The target Kubernetes version required add-on compatibility checks.
  • Some workloads depended on ingress and load balancer behavior.
  • Stateful components used persistent storage.
  • Monitoring and alerting needed to remain available throughout the change.
  • Old worker nodes could not be replaced all at once without risking capacity pressure.

docs.title: Standard Solution
docs.text:
The upgrade was modeled as a phased migration:

  1. Build a baseline inventory of workloads, nodes, ingress, PVCs, add-ons, and alerts.
  2. Freeze unrelated infrastructure changes during the upgrade window.
  3. Upgrade the control plane using the platform-supported method.
  4. Upgrade core add-ons to versions compatible with the target Kubernetes version.
  5. Create a new worker node group with the target runtime and image.
  6. Migrate workloads by cordoning and draining old nodes in small batches.
  7. Validate application health after each batch.
  8. Keep old nodes available until the observation window passes.
  9. Remove old nodes after metrics and business checks remain stable.

docs.title: Validation Model
docs.text:
The team used three validation layers:

Layer Examples Cluster health API readiness, node readiness, system pods, events. Workload health Pod readiness, rollout status, logs, probes, job completion. Business smoke tests Login, core API calls, scheduled jobs, external callbacks, dashboard visibility.

docs.title: Rollback Model
docs.text:
The control plane upgrade itself was treated as forward-only. Rollback protection came from:

  • Keeping old worker nodes until workload validation passed.
  • Migrating workloads gradually.
  • Preserving manifest snapshots.
  • Maintaining application rollback ability.
  • Avoiding irreversible storage changes during the same window.

docs.title: Reusable Lessons
docs.text:
• Add-on compatibility often causes more practical risk than the control plane version change.
  • New node groups provide a cleaner rollback boundary than in-place node mutation.
  • Ingress, CSI drivers, and monitoring agents deserve explicit test cases.
  • PVC capacity should be checked before migration to avoid confusing upgrade issues with storage pressure.
  • Every critical alert should link to a runbook before the maintenance window.

docs.title: Related Standards
docs.text:
• EKS and Kubernetes Upgrade Standard
  • NFS PVC Capacity Management
  • Prometheus Alerting Standard

docs.title: Case Type
docs.text: Cross-provider object storage migration.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
An engineering team needed to move application files from one object storage provider to another while preserving application availability and minimizing data inconsistency.

All identifiers are generic. No original bucket names, domains, regions, customer data, or access credentials are preserved.

docs.title: Standard Solution
docs.text:
The migration used an incremental sync model:

  1. Inventory source buckets and prefixes.
  2. Estimate object count and total size.
  3. Create target buckets and access policies.
  4. Run initial bulk sync.
  5. Compare object counts and sample checksums.
  6. Run incremental sync for changed objects.
  7. Switch application reads to target storage.
  8. Switch application writes to target storage.
  9. Keep source storage read-only during rollback window.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
• Object count comparison.
  • Total size comparison.
  • Sample checksum validation.
  • Application read smoke test.
  • Upload smoke test.
  • Access policy review.

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text: Rollback is easiest before write traffic moves to the target. After target writes begin, rollback requires either reverse sync or application-level reconciliation.

docs.title: Reusable Lessons
docs.text:
• Count and size checks are not enough; sample checksums and application reads are needed.
  • Write cutover is the true point of risk.
  • Temporary credentials should be removed immediately after migration.
  • Prefix mapping must be reviewed before the first sync.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Cloud Network and Object Storage Migration Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes how to operate Argo CD as a GitOps deployment controller for Kubernetes.

docs.title: GitOps Principles
docs.text:
1. Git is the desired-state source.
  2. Cluster changes should flow through reviewed commits.
  3. Secrets should be referenced through a secret management process, not stored in plain Git.
  4. Applications should have clear sync policies.
  5. Notifications should be routed without exposing webhook secrets.

docs.title: Installation Model
docs.text:
Use the official Argo CD manifests or Helm chart. For production, prefer high availability mode and separate the Argo CD namespace from application namespaces.

Validation:

kubectl get pods -n argocd
kubectl get svc -n argocd
kubectl get applications -n argocd

docs.title: Application Definition
docs.text:
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: example-service
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/example/example-repo.git
    targetRevision: main
    path: deploy/kubernetes
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: example-namespace
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: false
      selfHeal: true

docs.title: Sync Policy Guidance
docs.text: Policy Use When Manual sync Production or high-risk services. Automated sync without prune Lower-risk services where deletion should remain manual. Automated sync with prune Mature services with strong review and rollback controls.

docs.title: Notification Standard
docs.text:
Notifications should include:

  • Application name.
  • Sync status.
  • Health status.
  • Revision.
  • Environment.
  • Link to runbook or dashboard.

Never commit notification webhook URLs or chat tokens in plain text.

docs.title: Backup and Restore
docs.text:
Export Argo CD application state:

argocd admin export > argocd-export.yaml

Store exports securely. Review exports before sharing because repository URLs, project names, and cluster references can reveal internal topology.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom Check Application out of sync Git revision, generated manifests, ignored differences Sync fails RBAC, namespace existence, CRDs, admission policies Health unknown Resource health customization, CRD status fields Repo connection fails Repository credentials, network, known hosts

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes a safe Jenkins pipeline pattern for building container images and deploying to Kubernetes.

docs.title: Pipeline Stages
docs.text:
Use a predictable stage model:

checkout
test
build
scan
package
push image
deploy to staging
smoke test
manual approval
deploy to production
post-deploy verification

docs.title: Required Inputs
docs.text: Input Example Git repository https://github.com/example/example-repo.git Branch main Image registry.example.com/example/app Version Git SHA or release tag Kubernetes namespace example-namespace Deployment name example-service Environment staging or production

docs.title: Credential Handling
docs.text:
Store credentials in Jenkins credentials or an external secret manager:

  • Git credentials.
  • Registry credentials.
  • Kubernetes deploy credentials.
  • Notification webhook.
  • Cloud credentials.

Never hardcode credentials in Jenkinsfiles, shell scripts, or public documentation.

docs.title: Example Jenkinsfile Skeleton
docs.text:
pipeline {
  agent any

  environment {
    IMAGE = 'registry.example.com/example/app'
    NAMESPACE = 'example-namespace'
    DEPLOYMENT = 'example-service'
  }

  stages {
    stage('Checkout') {
      steps {
        checkout scm
      }
    }

    stage('Test') {
      steps {
        sh 'make test'
      }
    }

    stage('Build Image') {
      steps {
        sh 'docker build -t ${IMAGE}:${GIT_COMMIT} .'
      }
    }

    stage('Push Image') {
      steps {
        withCredentials([usernamePassword(credentialsId: 'registry-credentials', usernameVariable: 'REGISTRY_USER', passwordVariable: 'REGISTRY_PASSWORD')]) {
          sh 'echo "$REGISTRY_PASSWORD" | docker login registry.example.com -u "$REGISTRY_USER" --password-stdin'
          sh 'docker push ${IMAGE}:${GIT_COMMIT}'
        }
      }
    }

    stage('Deploy') {
      steps {
        sh 'kubectl -n ${NAMESPACE} set image deployment/${DEPLOYMENT} app=${IMAGE}:${GIT_COMMIT}'
        sh 'kubectl -n ${NAMESPACE} rollout status deployment/${DEPLOYMENT} --timeout=5m'
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Kubernetes Deploy Safety
docs.text:
Before production deploy:

  • Confirm image tag is immutable.
  • Confirm Kubernetes context and namespace.
  • Confirm rollout strategy and readiness probes.
  • Confirm rollback command.
  • Confirm monitoring dashboard and alert ownership.

Rollback:

kubectl -n example-namespace rollout undo deployment/example-service
kubectl -n example-namespace rollout status deployment/example-service

docs.title: Notification Standard
docs.text:
Notifications should include:

  • Job name.
  • Build number.
  • Git commit.
  • Environment.
  • Deployment status.
  • Runbook link.

Do not include webhook URLs, credentials, or sensitive logs in notification templates.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This runbook describes how to roll back a Kubernetes Deployment after a failed release.

docs.title: Trigger
docs.text: Use this runbook when a release causes elevated errors, failed readiness, broken smoke tests, or unacceptable latency.

docs.title: Initial Triage
docs.text:
kubectl get deployment example-service -n example-namespace
kubectl rollout status deployment/example-service -n example-namespace
kubectl get pods -n example-namespace -l app=example-service
kubectl get events -n example-namespace --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'

Check recent revisions:

kubectl rollout history deployment/example-service -n example-namespace

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text:
Rollback to the previous revision:

kubectl rollout undo deployment/example-service -n example-namespace
kubectl rollout status deployment/example-service -n example-namespace --timeout=5m

Rollback to a specific revision:

kubectl rollout undo deployment/example-service -n example-namespace --to-revision=2

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
kubectl get pods -n example-namespace -l app=example-service
curl -sS https://service.example.com/health

Confirm:

  • Pods are ready.
  • Error rate returns to normal.
  • Latency returns to normal.
  • Business smoke tests pass.
  • No database or schema rollback is required.

docs.title: When Rollback Is Not Enough
docs.text:
Stop and escalate if:

  • The release included irreversible database migration.
  • The previous image is unavailable.
  • Config or secret changes caused the incident.
  • External dependencies changed at the same time.

docs.title: Closure Criteria
docs.text:
• Service recovered.
  • Failed release is blocked from redeploying automatically.
  • Root cause investigation is opened.
  • Follow-up fix has an owner.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes safe GitLab operations for backups, CI runners, and deployment repositories.

docs.title: Controls
docs.text:
• Back up repositories and metadata.
  • Protect default branches.
  • Store CI/CD variables securely.
  • Use scoped deploy tokens.
  • Rotate runner registration tokens.
  • Review pipeline permissions.

docs.title: Backup Validation
docs.text: Do not trust backup creation alone. Restore to a test instance on a schedule and validate repository access.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Jenkins to Kubernetes Pipeline Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes safe operation of bastion hosts or JumpServer-style access platforms.

docs.title: Access Principles
docs.text:
• Use named user accounts.
  • Enforce MFA where possible.
  • Record sessions for privileged access.
  • Grant least privilege.
  • Rotate administrative credentials.
  • Avoid direct production SSH access outside the bastion.

docs.title: Operational Checklist
docs.text:
• Admin access is restricted.
  • Asset inventory is current.
  • User groups map to roles.
  • Session recording is enabled.
  • Emergency access is documented.
  • Logs are retained and reviewed.

docs.title: Publication Safety
docs.text: Do not publish real asset lists, usernames, internal IPs, domains, session logs, or access policies.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • OpenVPN Access Standard

docs.title: Publication Safety Checklist
docs.text: Use this checklist before moving any document into a public repository or assistant dataset.

docs.title: File-Level Checks
docs.text:
• The document was rewritten for public use and is not a raw production artifact.
  • The document does not include real credentials, tokens, private keys, certificates, or session values.
  • The document does not include real customer names, personal names, personal contact data, or employment records.
  • The document does not include real internal domains, public IPs, private IP ranges, service names, namespaces, image registries, or repository URLs from historical environments.
  • The document does not include real database names, database hosts, SQL dumps, schema exports with business context, or business data.
  • The document does not include real alert webhook URLs, chat IDs, bot tokens, SMTP usernames, or mail app passwords.

docs.title: Architecture and Case Checks
docs.text:
• Internal diagrams have been redrawn with generic labels.
  • Project names have been replaced with generic names.
  • Environment names such as production, staging, and region-specific labels have been generalized.
  • The case focuses on the engineering pattern, not the original business.
  • The case includes validation and rollback, not only implementation steps.

docs.title: Command and Config Checks
docs.text:
• Commands use placeholder domains such as example.com.
  • Commands use placeholder IPs from documentation ranges such as 203.0.113.10.
  • Kubernetes examples use example-namespace, example-service, and example-cluster.
  • Container images use registry.example.com/example/app:1.0.0.
  • Cloud identifiers use placeholder account, VPC, subnet, and role names.
  • Secrets are represented as references to secret managers or placeholders.

docs.title: Assistant Dataset Checks
docs.text:
• Each Q&A answer cites a public-safe source document.
  • Answers do not include sensitive operational values.
  • Answers are concise and retrieval-friendly.
  • Questions are generic and reusable.
  • The dataset does not encode private environment names in tags or source fields.

docs.title: Required Scan
docs.text:
Run:

rg -n -i '(password|passwd|secret|token|private key|BEGIN .*PRIVATE KEY|AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|authorization:|bearer |smtp|telegram|webhook|prod-|\\.key|\\.pem)' public-devops-kb

Review every match. Matches in policy documents may be valid. Matches in public how-to documents require careful review.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes safe deployment and operation of Redash-style BI tools.

docs.title: Controls
docs.text:
• Protect admin access.
  • Store data source credentials securely.
  • Restrict query permissions.
  • Back up metadata database.
  • Review public dashboard sharing.

docs.title: Security Notes
docs.text: BI tools can expose production data through queries and dashboards. Do not publish data source credentials, query results, or customer data.

docs.title: Related Documents

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes Elasticsearch, Logstash, Filebeat, and Kibana style logging stacks.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• Use structured logs.
  • Keep index retention controlled.
  • Restrict Kibana access.
  • Avoid indexing secrets or personal data.
  • Monitor disk usage and index health.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text: curl -sS http://elasticsearch.example.internal:9200/_cluster/health

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Loki Logging Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines how to add Prometheus exporters for services such as MySQL, Redis, MongoDB, Nginx, JVM applications, Node Exporter, and blackbox monitoring.

docs.title: Exporter Principles
docs.text:
1. Run exporters close to the target service.
  2. Use least-privilege metrics accounts.
  3. Avoid exposing exporters publicly.
  4. Add labels that identify service, environment, and owner.
  5. Pair every exporter with dashboards and alert rules.

docs.title: Exporter Checklist
docs.text: Exporter Key Checks Node Exporter CPU, memory, disk, filesystem, network. MySQL Exporter Connection, replication, slow queries, buffer pool. Redis Exporter Memory, clients, persistence, replication, keyspace. MongoDB Exporter Connections, locks, replication, storage. Nginx Exporter Requests, connections, status endpoint. JVM Exporter Heap, GC, threads, class loading. Blackbox Exporter HTTP, TCP, DNS, TLS expiry.

docs.title: Scrape Configuration Pattern
docs.text:
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: example-service
    static_configs:
      - targets:
          - exporter.example.internal:9100
        labels:
          service: example-service
          team: platform

Do not publish real internal targets.

docs.title: Alert Coverage
docs.text:
Minimum alert categories:

  • Exporter down.
  • Target down.
  • Disk capacity.
  • Memory pressure.
  • Error rate.
  • Replication lag.
  • TLS expiry.
  • JVM GC pressure.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
curl -sS http://exporter.example.internal:9100/metrics | head

In Prometheus:

up{job="example-service"}

docs.title: Security Notes
docs.text:
• Exporters may expose service metadata.
  • Restrict exporter ports to Prometheus.
  • Use TLS or private networking where required.
  • Store database metrics credentials in a secret manager.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Prometheus Alerting Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines basic Node Exporter deployment and alert coverage.

docs.title: Metrics To Use
docs.text:
• CPU saturation.
  • Memory pressure.
  • Disk capacity.
  • Filesystem errors.
  • Network errors.
  • Load average.

docs.title: Deployment Notes
docs.text:
Run Node Exporter on each Linux host and restrict access to Prometheus.

Validation:

curl -sS http://node.example.internal:9100/metrics | head

docs.title: Application Maintenance and Health Check Standard
Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines a reusable model for application health checks and maintenance runbooks.

docs.title: Health Check Layers
docs.text: Layer Examples Infrastructure Host, CPU, memory, disk, network. Runtime Process, container, pod, JVM, worker. Application HTTP health endpoint, queue consumer, scheduler. Dependency Database, cache, object storage, third-party API. User Journey Login, core API call, report generation, callback.

docs.title: Maintenance Workflow
docs.text:
1. Announce maintenance window.
  2. Confirm backup and rollback.
  3. Pause risky automated jobs.
  4. Apply change.
  5. Run technical validation.
  6. Run business smoke tests.
  7. Monitor for the observation window.
  8. Close with summary and follow-up items.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• Kubernetes Deployment Rollback Runbook
  • Prometheus Alerting Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This runbook guides an operator through responding to a Kubernetes PVC usage alert.

docs.title: Alert Names
docs.text:
• KubernetesPVCUsageHigh
  • KubernetesPVCUsageCritical

docs.title: Initial Triage
docs.text:
Identify the affected PVC:

kubectl get pvc -A | grep <pvc-name>
kubectl describe pvc <pvc-name> -n <namespace>
kubectl get pod -n <namespace> -o wide

Check whether the application is healthy:

kubectl get pods -n <namespace>
kubectl get events -n <namespace> --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'

docs.title: Confirm Usage
docs.text:
If kubelet volume metrics are available, check dashboard or Prometheus:

kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes / kubelet_volume_stats_capacity_bytes * 100

For NFS-backed storage, confirm on the storage host or observation mount:

df -h <storage-root>
du -sh <storage-root>/<namespace>/<pvc-name>

docs.title: Determine Impact
docs.text:
Ask:

  • Is the application currently failing writes?
  • Is usage increasing quickly?
  • Is the PVC used by a stateful service?
  • Is the data business-critical?
  • Is there a recent release or job that explains growth?

docs.title: Path A: Temporary or Disposable Data
docs.text:
1. Confirm the directory contains disposable files.
  2. Run cleanup in dry-run mode if available.
  3. Delete only files matching approved retention rules.
  4. Recheck usage and application health.

docs.title: Path B: Business-Critical Data
docs.text:
1. Notify the service owner.
  2. Confirm backup or snapshot status.
  3. Expand the volume if supported.
  4. If expansion is not supported, prepare migration to a larger volume.
  5. Avoid manual deletion unless explicitly approved.

docs.title: Path C: Rapid Unexpected Growth
docs.text:
1. Identify the writer process or workload.
  2. Check recent deployments, batch jobs, and logs.
  3. Stop or throttle the source of growth if risk is critical.
  4. Expand or clean up after the growth source is controlled.

docs.title: Expansion Example
docs.text:
If the StorageClass supports expansion:

kubectl patch pvc <pvc-name> -n <namespace> \
  -p '{"spec":{"resources":{"requests":{"storage":"200Gi"}}}}'

Validate:

kubectl get pvc <pvc-name> -n <namespace>
kubectl describe pvc <pvc-name> -n <namespace>

docs.title: Closure Criteria
docs.text:
Close the incident only when:

  • Usage is below the warning threshold or expansion is complete.
  • Application read/write checks pass.
  • Owner confirms no data loss.
  • Monitoring returns to normal.
  • A follow-up task exists for retention or capacity planning if needed.

docs.title: Post-Incident Notes
docs.text:
Record:

  • Root cause.
  • Peak usage.
  • Remediation action.
  • Prevention item.
  • Whether alert thresholds need adjustment.

docs.title: Linux Disk and LVM Expansion Runbook
Purpose
docs.text: This runbook describes how to safely expand Linux storage using a new disk, partition, LVM, and filesystem growth.

docs.title: Safety Warning
docs.text: Disk operations can destroy data. Do not run formatting, partitioning, or LVM commands until the target disk is confirmed and approved.

docs.title: Initial Checks
docs.text:
lsblk
blkid
df -hT
sudo pvs
sudo vgs
sudo lvs

Identify:

  • New disk name.
  • Existing volume group.
  • Root or data logical volume.
  • Filesystem type.
  • Backup status.

docs.title: Add a New Disk to LVM
docs.text:
Example target disk:

/dev/sdb

Create a physical volume:

sudo pvcreate /dev/sdb

Extend volume group:

sudo vgextend example_vg /dev/sdb

Extend logical volume:

sudo lvextend -l +100%FREE /dev/example_vg/example_lv

docs.title: Grow Filesystem
docs.text:
For XFS:

sudo xfs_growfs /

For ext4:

sudo resize2fs /dev/example_vg/example_lv

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
df -hT
sudo pvs
sudo vgs
sudo lvs

Application validation:

  • Service can write to the expanded path.
  • Monitoring sees the new capacity.
  • No filesystem errors appear in dmesg.

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text: LVM expansion rollback is not always simple. The safe rollback is a verified backup restore. For this reason, backup validation is required before the change.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Linux Server Baseline Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines how shell scripts should be cleaned and published as reusable DevOps automation.

docs.title: Script Requirements
docs.text:
• Use set -euo pipefail where compatible.
  • Validate required commands.
  • Provide dry-run for destructive actions.
  • Log actions.
  • Avoid hardcoded credentials, IPs, domains, and personal paths.
  • Make thresholds configurable.

docs.title: Public Template
docs.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

main() {
  echo "run safe operation"
}

main "$@"

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Publication Safety Checklist

docs.title: Cloud Network and Object Storage Migration Standard
Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes safe planning for cloud network connectivity and object storage migration across providers or accounts.

docs.title: Network Planning
docs.text:
Document:

  • Source and target VPC or VNet CIDRs.
  • Subnet layout.
  • Routing tables.
  • NAT and egress design.
  • Security groups or firewall policies.
  • DNS resolution.
  • Private connectivity such as VPN, peering, transit gateway, or cloud connect.

Use placeholder identifiers in public documents:

vpc-00000000000000000
subnet-00000000000000000
10.0.0.0/16
203.0.113.10

docs.title: Connectivity Validation
docs.text:
ping 10.0.0.10
traceroute 10.0.0.10
curl -I https://service.example.com
dig service.example.com

For private services, prefer application-level checks over raw ping.

docs.title: Object Storage Migration
docs.text:
Before migration:

  • Estimate object count and total size.
  • Check object naming and prefix strategy.
  • Validate permissions.
  • Decide metadata preservation requirements.
  • Plan incremental sync.
  • Define cutover and rollback.

Validation:

  • Object count comparison.
  • Size comparison.
  • Sample checksum comparison.
  • Application read test.
  • Access policy test.

docs.title: Security Notes
docs.text:
• Do not publish access keys or secret keys.
  • Use temporary roles where possible.
  • Restrict migration permissions to required buckets and prefixes.
  • Encrypt objects at rest and in transit.
  • Remove migration credentials after cutover.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Publication Safety Checklist

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes safe use of BuildKit for container image builds.

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Faster Dockerfile builds.
  • Build cache optimization.
  • Multi-platform image builds.
  • CI/CD image pipelines.

docs.title: Baseline
docs.text:
buildctl --version
docker buildx version

docs.title: Build Safety
docs.text:
• Do not bake secrets into image layers.
  • Use secret mounts for build-time credentials.
  • Pin base image versions.
  • Scan output images.
  • Push only to approved registries.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text: docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64 -t registry.example.com/example/app:1.0.0 .

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• Docker Host Operations Standard
  • Jenkins to Kubernetes Pipeline Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes safe operation of ChartMuseum or a similar private Helm chart repository.

docs.title: Controls
docs.text:
• Enable authentication.
  • Use TLS.
  • Back up chart storage.
  • Apply chart retention policies.
  • Restrict publishing permission.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
helm repo add example https://charts.example.com
helm search repo example

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines how to operate small or transitional service stacks with Docker Compose without publishing environment-specific secrets or runtime data.

docs.title: When To Use
docs.text: Use Docker Compose for development, demos, low-risk internal tools, or migration staging. Use Kubernetes or managed services for production workloads that require scheduling, autoscaling, or high availability.

docs.title: Stack Checklist
docs.text:
• Pin image versions.
  • Put persistent data in named volumes or documented bind mounts.
  • Add restart policies.
  • Configure log size limits.
  • Keep .env files out of public repositories.
  • Document backup and restore for every stateful service.
  • Review exposed ports.

docs.title: Compose Pattern
docs.text:
services:
  app:
    image: registry.example.com/example/app:1.0.0
    restart: unless-stopped
    env_file:
      - .env
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    logging:
      driver: json-file
      options:
        max-size: "100m"
        max-file: "3"

Do not publish the actual .env file. Publish .env.example with placeholder values only.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
docker compose config
docker compose ps
docker compose logs --tail=100 app

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Docker Host Operations Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes public-safe etcd operations for Kubernetes and distributed systems.

docs.title: Operating Principles
docs.text:
• Treat etcd as critical state.
  • Use an odd number of members for quorum.
  • Back up before cluster changes.
  • Monitor leader changes, disk latency, database size, and alarms.
  • Protect peer and client certificates.

docs.title: Backup
docs.text:
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save snapshot.db

Store snapshots encrypted and test restore regularly.

docs.title: Health Checks
docs.text:
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl endpoint health
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl endpoint status --write-out=table

docs.title: Kubernetes Notes
docs.text: For managed Kubernetes, control plane etcd is usually provider-managed. For self-managed clusters, etcd backup and restore must be part of the platform runbook.

docs.title: Publication Safety
docs.text: Do not publish real etcd certificates, keys, endpoints, snapshots, or cluster member names.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• kubeadm Installation Standard
  • Kubernetes Cluster Provisioning Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes safe MongoDB deployment, monitoring, backup, and migration practices.

docs.title: Baseline
docs.text:
• Enable authentication.
  • Bind only to private interfaces.
  • Use replica sets for availability.
  • Back up with a documented restore process.
  • Monitor connections, replication lag, locks, storage, and memory.

docs.title: Backup
docs.text:
mongodump --host mongo.example.internal --out /backup/mongodb

Store backups encrypted and test restore regularly.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text: mongosh --host mongo.example.internal --eval 'rs.status()'

docs.title: Security Notes
docs.text: Do not publish MongoDB connection strings, users, passwords, or internal hosts.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Prometheus Exporter Monitoring Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines public-safe MySQL installation, backup, migration, and recovery practices.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: Use this for self-managed MySQL, cloud RDS-style MySQL, and migration planning. Provider-specific commands should be placed in cloud-specific appendices.

docs.title: Operating Principles
docs.text:
1. Prefer managed database services when operational maturity is limited.
  2. Separate administrative accounts from application accounts.
  3. Never publish initial root passwords, generated credentials, or connection strings.
  4. Test backup restore, not only backup creation.
  5. Use controlled maintenance windows for major upgrades and migrations.

docs.title: Installation Baseline
docs.text:
For self-managed MySQL:

  • Use a supported MySQL version.
  • Create a dedicated mysql system user.
  • Store data on a dedicated volume.
  • Set explicit character set and collation.
  • Configure slow query logging.
  • Enable service management through systemd.

Example configuration concepts:

[mysqld]
datadir=/var/lib/mysql
socket=/var/lib/mysql/mysql.sock
character-set-server=utf8mb4
collation-server=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
slow_query_log=ON
long_query_time=1
max_connections=500

docs.title: Account Model
docs.text:
Use least privilege:

CREATE USER 'app_user'@'10.0.0.%' IDENTIFIED BY '<REDACTED_PASSWORD>';
GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON example_db.* TO 'app_user'@'10.0.0.%';
FLUSH PRIVILEGES;

The password placeholder must be replaced through a secret manager, not committed to a repository.

docs.title: Backup Standard
docs.text:
Use both logical and physical backup when appropriate.

Logical backup:

mysqldump --single-transaction --routines --triggers --events example_db > example_db.sql

Physical backup tools are better for larger datasets. Always document:

  • Backup type.
  • Backup schedule.
  • Retention period.
  • Encryption method.
  • Restore test frequency.
  • Owner and escalation path.

docs.title: Migration Standard
docs.text:
Before migration:

  • Confirm source and target versions.
  • Check character set and collation.
  • Estimate data size and downtime.
  • Validate network throughput.
  • Freeze schema changes.
  • Prepare rollback plan.

Migration validation:

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM important_table;
SHOW SLAVE STATUS\G
SHOW REPLICA STATUS\G

Use checksums or application-level validation for critical tables.

docs.title: Root Password Reset
docs.text:
Root password reset is a break-glass procedure. It should require:

  • Approval.
  • Maintenance window.
  • Console or host-level access.
  • Audit record.
  • Immediate credential rotation after recovery.

Do not publish environment-specific reset passwords or internal access paths.

docs.title: Benchmarking
docs.text:
Use benchmarking to compare configurations, not to prove production capacity in isolation.

Example:

sysbench oltp_read_write --mysql-host=db.example.internal --mysql-user=example-user --mysql-db=example_db prepare
sysbench oltp_read_write --mysql-host=db.example.internal --mysql-user=example-user --mysql-db=example_db run

Provide the database password through a protected client configuration or secret injection mechanism rather than a command-line argument.

Avoid running benchmarks against production without explicit approval.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Publication Safety Checklist

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines installation, persistence, migration, and cluster practices for Redis.

docs.title: Operating Principles
docs.text:
1. Disable Transparent Huge Pages for Redis hosts.
  2. Set Linux overcommit and file descriptor limits deliberately.
  3. Protect Redis with network isolation and authentication.
  4. Choose RDB, AOF, or mixed persistence based on recovery requirements.
  5. Test migration and restore procedures before production use.

docs.title: Host Tuning
docs.text:
echo never | sudo tee /sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/enabled
echo 'vm.overcommit_memory = 1' | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/99-redis.conf
sudo sysctl --system

Set limits:

redis soft nofile 65535
redis hard nofile 65535

docs.title: Single Instance Baseline
docs.text:
Recommended configuration concepts:

bind 127.0.0.1 10.0.0.10
protected-mode yes
port 6379
dir /var/lib/redis
appendonly yes
appendfsync everysec
maxmemory-policy noeviction

Store credentials in a secret manager. Do not commit requirepass values.

docs.title: Persistence Choices
docs.text: Mode Use Case Trade-Off RDB Periodic snapshots Faster restart, possible data loss between snapshots. AOF Better durability Larger files and rewrite management required. Mixed Balanced durability and restore speed Requires version support and testing.

docs.title: AOF Migration Procedure
docs.text:
1. Confirm Redis version compatibility.
  2. Stop writes or enter a controlled maintenance window.
  3. Generate or rewrite AOF on the source.
  4. Copy AOF to the target through a secure channel.
  5. Verify checksum.
  6. Start target Redis.
  7. Validate keys, memory, persistence status, and application behavior.

Example checksum:

sha256sum appendonly.aof

docs.title: Cluster Migration Notes
docs.text:
For Redis Cluster:

  • Preserve slot ownership information.
  • Migrate one shard or replica group at a time.
  • Validate cluster_state:ok.
  • Confirm application clients support cluster topology refresh.
  • Keep rollback data until post-migration checks pass.

Validation:

redis-cli -h 10.0.0.10 -p 6379 INFO persistence
redis-cli -h 10.0.0.10 -p 6379 INFO replication
redis-cli -h 10.0.0.10 -p 6379 CLUSTER INFO

docs.title: Security Notes
docs.text:
• Do not expose Redis directly to the public internet.
  • Restrict access by security group, firewall, or network policy.
  • Use TLS where supported and required.
  • Rotate credentials after migration or emergency access.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• Linux Server Baseline Standard
  • Publication Safety Checklist

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines safe Terraform usage for infrastructure as code.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• Store state remotely and encrypted.
  • Lock state during applies.
  • Keep secrets out of .tf files and state where possible.
  • Use modules for repeated infrastructure.
  • Review plans before apply.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
terraform fmt
terraform validate
terraform plan
terraform apply

docs.title: Publication Safety
docs.text: Do not publish real account IDs, resource IDs, backend bucket names, state files, or provider credentials.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Cloud Network and Object Storage Migration Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines a safe baseline for preparing Linux servers for DevOps workloads. It is intended for new servers, rebuilds, and post-provisioning hardening.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: The baseline applies to common Linux distributions such as Rocky Linux, CentOS-compatible systems, Ubuntu, and Debian. Provider-specific steps should be handled in separate cloud build templates.

docs.title: Baseline Principles
docs.text:
1. Make changes idempotent where possible.
  2. Keep access control separate from application deployment.
  3. Prefer SSH key authentication over password login.
  4. Record every non-default system setting.
  5. Avoid embedding personal users, public keys, passwords, or internal mirrors in public scripts.
  6. Validate the server after each major configuration block.

docs.title: 1. Identify System
docs.text:
cat /etc/os-release
uname -a
hostnamectl

Record distribution, kernel, architecture, timezone, hostname, and primary network interface.

docs.title: 2. Update Packages
docs.text:
For RHEL-compatible systems:

sudo dnf makecache
sudo dnf update -y
sudo dnf install -y vim curl wget tar unzip net-tools lsof sysstat chrony bash-completion

For Debian-compatible systems:

sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get upgrade -y
sudo apt-get install -y vim curl wget tar unzip net-tools lsof sysstat chrony bash-completion

docs.title: 3. Configure Time Synchronization
docs.text:
sudo systemctl enable --now chronyd || sudo systemctl enable --now chrony
timedatectl
chronyc sources -v

Use the timezone required by the service owner. For public examples:

sudo timedatectl set-timezone UTC

docs.title: 4. Configure SSH Access
docs.text:
Recommended settings:

PermitRootLogin no
PasswordAuthentication no
PubkeyAuthentication yes
UseDNS no
GSSAPIAuthentication no

Validation:

sudo sshd -t
sudo systemctl reload sshd || sudo systemctl reload ssh

Never publish real SSH public keys or usernames in public scripts. Use placeholders such as example-user and ssh-ed25519 AAAA... devops@example.com.

docs.title: 5. Configure Command History
docs.text:
Recommended:

cat <<'EOF' | sudo tee /etc/profile.d/history-policy.sh
export HISTTIMEFORMAT='%F %T '
export HISTSIZE=10000
export HISTFILESIZE=20000
EOF

Do not rely on shell history as a security audit log. Use centralized audit logging for regulated systems.

docs.title: 6. Configure Kernel and Limits
docs.text:
Common baseline:

cat <<'EOF' | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/99-devops-baseline.conf
net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1
net.ipv4.tcp_tw_reuse = 1
vm.swappiness = 10
fs.file-max = 1048576
EOF

sudo sysctl --system

For service users:

cat <<'EOF' | sudo tee /etc/security/limits.d/99-devops-baseline.conf
* soft nofile 65535
* hard nofile 65535
* soft nproc 65535
* hard nproc 65535
EOF

docs.title: 7. Configure Firewall
docs.text:
Use a default-deny inbound model where possible. Open only documented ports.

For firewalld:

sudo systemctl enable --now firewalld
sudo firewall-cmd --get-active-zones
sudo firewall-cmd --add-service=ssh --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --reload

For cloud servers, align OS firewall rules with cloud security groups.

docs.title: 8. Prepare Data Disks
docs.text:
Before formatting a disk:

lsblk
blkid
df -hT

Only format disks that have been explicitly approved and are not mounted. See Linux Disk and LVM Expansion Runbook.

docs.title: Validation Checklist
docs.text:
• System packages updated.
  • Time sync enabled and healthy.
  • SSH config validates.
  • Firewall enabled or explicitly managed by cloud security groups.
  • Baseline sysctl settings applied.
  • Limits file installed where required.
  • Disk layout documented.
  • No plaintext credentials stored in bootstrap scripts.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• Linux Disk and LVM Expansion Runbook
  • Docker Host Operations Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes using Supervisor for simple process management on Linux hosts.

docs.title: When To Use
docs.text: Use Supervisor for small legacy services or utilities. Prefer systemd or orchestrators for modern production workloads.

docs.title: Program Template

[program:example-service]
command=/opt/example/bin/start.sh
directory=/opt/example
autostart=true
autorestart=true
stdout_logfile=/var/log/example-service/out.log
stderr_logfile=/var/log/example-service/err.log

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
supervisorctl status
supervisorctl tail example-service

1. Inventory hosts.
  2. Review security advisories.
  3. Test patches in non-production.
  4. Schedule maintenance.
  5. Apply updates.
  6. Reboot if required.
  7. Validate services.

docs.title: Ubuntu 26.04 AI 全栈开发环境配置手册 v2.0
适用范围与约束声明
docs.text:
适用： - OS：Ubuntu 26.04 LTS Desktop（GNOME，X11 或 Wayland） - 硬件：x86_64，无 GPU，8GB+ RAM - 用途：个人 AI 应用开发工作站（Go + Python + Vue.js + Claude API + 向量数据库） - 执行身份：具备 sudo 权限的普通用户

不适用： - 服务器 / headless 环境 - NVIDIA GPU 场景（不覆盖 CUDA 安装） - 生产部署（本手册仅覆盖本地开发工作流） - 多人共享机器（secrets 管理策略需调整）

风险声明： - Claude Desktop Linux 版为社区非官方构建，Anthropic 不提供支持 - Ollama 在纯 CPU 环境下推理速度有限，仅适合开发期流程验证 - 本手册涉及修改 shell 配置、添加第三方 APT 仓库，建议在全新安装的系统上执行

预计耗时： 2.5–4 小时（含下载时间）

docs.title: 架构总览
docs.text:
开发工作站组件栈
├── 系统层
│   ├── Ubuntu 26.04 基础配置（禁休眠、中文输入、Swap）
│   └── Shell 环境（Bash + direnv + 统一 env 文件）
├── 工具层
│   ├── Git + SSH + 全局 .gitignore
│   ├── VSCode（官方 .deb）
│   └── tmux（终端复用）
├── 语言层
│   ├── Go（官方 tarball）
│   ├── Python（pyenv + uv）
│   └── Node.js（nvm + pnpm）
├── 容器层
│   └── Docker Engine + Compose（统一 docker-compose 管理本地服务）
├── 服务层（docker-compose 统一编排）
│   ├── Qdrant（向量数据库）
│   ├── PostgreSQL + pgvector（关系型 + 向量）
│   ├── Redis（缓存 / 会话 / 队列）
│   └── Langfuse（LLM 可观测性）
├── AI 客户端层
│   ├── Claude Code CLI（官方 Linux 路径）
│   ├── Claude Desktop（非官方 .deb，可选）
│   └── Ollama（本地 LLM，开发期成本控制）
└── 项目模板层
    ├── Makefile（工作流标准化）
    ├── .envrc（per-project 环境变量）
    └── docker-compose.yml（本地服务编排）

核心设计原则： - Shell 环境变量单一来源：~/.config/devenv/env.sh，~/.bashrc 只 source 一次 - 本地服务统一 docker-compose 管理，不直接 docker run - 开发期用 Ollama 验证流程，仅在集成测试和生产调用 Claude API，控制成本 - per-project 环境变量用 direnv 隔离，不污染全局

docs.title: Phase 0：预检
docs.text:
执行所有后续步骤前，先确认环境满足基础条件。

# 确认系统版本
lsb_release -a

# 确认是 GNOME 桌面
echo $XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP

# 检查可用磁盘空间（建议 /home 至少剩余 40GB）
df -h ~

# 检查内存
free -h

# 确认 sudo 可用
sudo whoami    # 应返回 root

# 确认网络（如需代理请提前配置）
curl -fsSL https://go.dev/VERSION?m=text

继续前提： 磁盘剩余 ≥ 40GB，RAM ≥ 8GB，网络可访问境外资源。

docs.title: 1.1 系统更新与基础工具
docs.text:
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y

sudo apt install -y \
  curl wget git build-essential \
  ca-certificates gnupg lsb-release \
  software-properties-common apt-transport-https \
  unzip zip jq tree htop net-tools \
  tmux xclip libfuse2 imagemagick p7zip-full icoutils

验证： 

git --version && curl --version && tmux -V

docs.title: 1.2 Swap 配置
docs.text:
为什么： 纯 CPU 运行 Embedding 模型或 Ollama 推理时，内存压力显著。没有 Swap 会触发 OOM Killer，进程被强制终止。

# 检查当前 Swap 状态
free -h && swapon --show

# 如果没有 Swap，创建 8GB Swapfile
if ! swapon --show | grep -q .; then
  sudo fallocate -l 8G /swapfile
  sudo chmod 600 /swapfile
  sudo mkswap /swapfile
  sudo swapon /swapfile
  echo '/swapfile none swap sw 0 0' | sudo tee -a /etc/fstab
  echo "Swapfile created"
fi

# 降低 swappiness（开发机不应频繁使用 Swap，只作保底）
echo 'vm.swappiness=10' | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/99-swappiness.conf
sudo sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/99-swappiness.conf

验证： 

free -h    # SWAP 行应显示 8G 可用
cat /proc/sys/vm/swappiness    # 应返回 10

docs.title: 1.3 禁用休眠与息屏
docs.text:
# systemd 层屏蔽所有休眠目标
sudo systemctl mask sleep.target suspend.target hibernate.target hybrid-sleep.target

# GNOME 层关闭息屏与自动挂起
gsettings set org.gnome.settings-daemon.plugins.power sleep-inactive-ac-type 'nothing'
gsettings set org.gnome.settings-daemon.plugins.power sleep-inactive-battery-type 'nothing'
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-enabled false
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false

验证： 

systemctl is-enabled sleep.target    # 应返回 masked
gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay    # 应返回 uint32 0

回滚（如需恢复休眠）： 

sudo systemctl unmask sleep.target suspend.target hibernate.target hybrid-sleep.target

docs.title: 1.4 中文输入法（Fcitx5）
docs.text:
为什么选 Fcitx5 而不是 IBus： Ubuntu 22.04 起 Fcitx5 已是 Linux 桌面首选输入框架，与 Wayland 兼容更好，中文扩展（拼音、五笔、Rime）更完整，社区维护更活跃。IBus 在 GNOME Wayland 下有已知的光标跟随问题。

sudo apt install -y \
  fcitx5 \
  fcitx5-chinese-addons \
  fcitx5-frontend-gtk4 \
  fcitx5-frontend-qt5 \
  im-config

# 设置为系统默认输入法框架
im-config -n fcitx5

# 设置开机自启
mkdir -p ~/.config/autostart
cat > ~/.config/autostart/fcitx5.desktop << 'EOF'
[Desktop Entry]
Name=Fcitx5
Exec=fcitx5
Terminal=false
Type=Application
Categories=System;Utility;
StartupNotify=false
EOF

注销并重新登录后： 

fcitx5 &
fcitx5-configtool    # 在 Input Method 中添加 Pinyin（拼音）

VSCode 中文输入问题（已知问题）：

VSCode 从应用菜单启动时不继承 Fcitx5 环境变量，导致无法输入中文。解决方法：

# 创建 VSCode 启动脚本，从终端启动以继承环境
cat > ~/.local/bin/code-fcitx << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
export GTK_IM_MODULE=fcitx
export QT_IM_MODULE=fcitx
export XMODIFIERS=@im=fcitx
exec code "$@"
EOF
chmod +x ~/.local/bin/code-fcitx

# 修改桌面快捷方式使用该脚本
sudo cp /usr/share/applications/code.desktop ~/.local/share/applications/
sed -i 's|Exec=/usr/share/code/code|Exec=/home/'"$USER"'/.local/bin/code-fcitx|g' \
  ~/.local/share/applications/code.desktop

常见失败模式： - Wayland 下输入法不跟随光标：Fcitx5 → Behavior → 勾选 "Show preedit in application window" - 切换快捷键冲突：默认 Ctrl+Space，可在 Fcitx5 Config → Global Options 中修改

docs.title: 1.5 Nerd Fonts
docs.text:
为什么： VSCode 和终端（tmux、oh-my-posh 等）使用图标字体，未安装 Nerd Fonts 会显示方块乱码。

mkdir -p ~/.local/share/fonts/JetBrainsMono

# 下载 JetBrainsMono Nerd Font（推荐：等宽、连字支持好）
wget -q "https://github.com/ryanoasis/nerd-fonts/releases/download/v3.2.1/JetBrainsMono.zip" \
  -O /tmp/JetBrainsMono.zip

unzip -o /tmp/JetBrainsMono.zip \
  "JetBrainsMonoNerdFont-Regular.ttf" \
  "JetBrainsMonoNerdFont-Bold.ttf" \
  "JetBrainsMonoNerdFont-Italic.ttf" \
  -d ~/.local/share/fonts/JetBrainsMono/

fc-cache -fv
rm /tmp/JetBrainsMono.zip

VSCode 中启用： Settings → editor.fontFamily → 'JetBrainsMono Nerd Font', monospace

docs.title: 1.6 tmux（终端复用）
docs.text:
为什么： AI 开发同时需要运行：后端服务、前端 dev server、docker compose 日志、LLM 推理进程。tmux 允许在一个终端窗口管理多个会话和窗格，且 SSH 断连后进程不丢失。

# 基础 tmux 配置（鼠标支持 + 合理快捷键）
cat > ~/.tmux.conf << 'EOF'
# 前缀键改为 Ctrl+a（比默认 Ctrl+b 更顺手）
unbind C-b
set-option -g prefix C-a
bind-key C-a send-prefix

# 鼠标支持
set -g mouse on

# 窗口从 1 开始编号（与键盘布局一致）
set -g base-index 1
setw -g pane-base-index 1

# 快捷键：垂直 / 水平分割
bind | split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind - split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# 增大历史记录行数
set -g history-limit 10000

# 256 色支持
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
EOF

常用快捷键参考：

操作 快捷键 垂直分割 Ctrl+a | 水平分割 Ctrl+a - 切换窗格 Ctrl+a 方向键 新建窗口 Ctrl+a c 切换窗口 Ctrl+a 数字 分离会话 Ctrl+a d 重连会话 tmux attach

docs.title: Phase 2：Shell 环境架构
docs.text: 为什么单独作为一个 Phase： 后续所有工具都需要修改环境变量。如果每个工具各自追加到 ~/.bashrc，会产生重复条目、顺序错乱、幂等性失效等问题。本 Phase 建立统一的环境变量管理架构，后续工具全部写入这个统一文件。

docs.title: 2.1 建立统一环境变量文件
docs.text:
# 创建目录结构
mkdir -p ~/.config/devenv
mkdir -p ~/.config/dev-secrets

# 创建统一 env 文件（所有工具的 PATH 和 export 集中在此）
cat > ~/.config/devenv/env.sh << 'EOF'
# ============================================================
# Dev Environment - 统一管理，工具安装时追加到此文件
# ============================================================

# Go
export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
export GOPATH=$HOME/go
export PATH=$PATH:$GOPATH/bin

# pyenv
export PYENV_ROOT="$HOME/.pyenv"
[[ -d $PYENV_ROOT/bin ]] && export PATH="$PYENV_ROOT/bin:$PATH"
command -v pyenv > /dev/null && eval "$(pyenv init -)"

# uv
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# nvm（通过 nvm.sh 加载）
export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] && source "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/bash_completion" ] && source "$NVM_DIR/bash_completion"

# direnv hook
command -v direnv > /dev/null && eval "$(direnv hook bash)"

# 本地 bin
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# Secrets（API Keys 等，不纳入 Git）
[ -f "$HOME/.config/dev-secrets/keys.env" ] && source "$HOME/.config/dev-secrets/keys.env"
EOF

# ~/.bashrc 只添加一行 source（幂等，添加前检查是否已存在）
grep -q 'devenv/env.sh' ~/.bashrc \
  || echo '[ -f "$HOME/.config/devenv/env.sh" ] && source "$HOME/.config/devenv/env.sh"' >> ~/.bashrc

source ~/.bashrc

docs.title: 2.2 API Key 管理
docs.text:
# 创建 secrets 文件（权限 600，仅当前用户可读）
cat > ~/.config/dev-secrets/keys.env << 'EOF'
# Anthropic
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="<ANTHROPIC_API_KEY>"

# OpenAI（如需）
# export OPENAI_API_KEY="<OPENAI_API_KEY>"

# 本地服务（无需保密，但统一放这里方便项目引用）
export QDRANT_HOST="localhost"
export QDRANT_PORT="6333"
export REDIS_URL="redis://localhost:6379"
export DATABASE_URL="postgresql://devuser:<LOCAL_DEV_POSTGRES_PASSWORD>@localhost:5432/devdb"
EOF

chmod 600 ~/.config/dev-secrets/keys.env
source ~/.bashrc

验证： 

echo $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | cut -c1-10    # 显示 Key 前 10 位

docs.title: 2.3 direnv（per-project 环境变量）
docs.text:
为什么： 全局 secrets 文件适合通用 Key，但不同项目有不同的模型选择、不同的服务端口、不同的 DB 名。direnv 实现进入目录自动加载、离开自动卸载，互不污染。

sudo apt install -y direnv
# direnv hook 已在 env.sh 中配置，直接生效
source ~/.bashrc

每个项目的用法： 

cd ~/projects/my-ai-app

cat > .envrc << 'EOF'
# 项目专属变量（覆盖全局）
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="<PROJECT_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY>"
export MODEL="claude-sonnet-4-6"
export QDRANT_COLLECTION="my-app-vectors"
export LOG_LEVEL="debug"
EOF

direnv allow .    # 首次允许加载
echo '.envrc' >> .gitignore    # 不提交 secrets

docs.title: 2.4 全局 .gitignore
docs.text:
git config --global core.excludesfile ~/.gitignore_global

cat > ~/.gitignore_global << 'EOF'
# Secrets
.env
.env.*
.envrc
*.key
*.pem
*.p12
*_rsa
*_ed25519

# Python
__pycache__/
*.py[cod]
*.pyo
.venv/
venv/
.pytest_cache/
*.egg-info/
dist/
build/

# Go
*.exe
*.test
vendor/

# Node.js
node_modules/
dist/
.nuxt/
.output/

# IDE
.idea/
.vscode/settings.json
*.swp
*.swo

# OS
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db

# Logs & Data
*.log
*.sqlite
*.db
EOF

docs.title: Phase 3：Git 与 SSH 配置
docs.text:
# Git 基础配置
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "you@example.com"
git config --global core.editor "code --wait"
git config --global init.defaultBranch main
git config --global pull.rebase false
git config --global push.autoSetupRemote true

# SSH Key 生成（如已有可跳过）
[ -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 ] || ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "you@example.com" -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

# SSH config 模板（多账户 / 多服务器场景）
mkdir -p ~/.ssh && chmod 700 ~/.ssh
cat > ~/.ssh/config << 'EOF'
# GitHub 主账户
Host github.com
  HostName github.com
  User git
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
  AddKeysToAgent yes

# 开发服务器（按需填写）
# Host dev-server
#   HostName 192.168.1.xxx
#   User ubuntu
#   IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
#   ServerAliveInterval 60
EOF
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/config

# 输出公钥，复制到 GitHub → Settings → SSH Keys
echo ""
echo "=== 你的 SSH 公钥（复制到 GitHub）==="
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

验证（添加 SSH Key 到 GitHub 后）： 

ssh -T git@github.com    # 应返回 "Hi username! You've successfully authenticated"

docs.title: Phase 4：VSCode
docs.text:
为什么用官方 .deb 而不是 Snap： Snap 版 VSCode 运行在沙箱中，对 /home 之外的路径、系统 socket、Go/Python 工具链的访问有限制，会导致 LSP 路径解析失败。官方 .deb 无此限制。

curl -fsSL https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc \
  | gpg --dearmor \
  | sudo tee /usr/share/keyrings/packages.microsoft.gpg > /dev/null

echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/packages.microsoft.gpg] \
  https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/code stable main" \
  | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vscode.list

sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y code
code --version

VSCode 核心配置（Ctrl+Shift+P → "Open User Settings JSON"）：

{
  "editor.fontFamily": "'JetBrainsMono Nerd Font', 'Courier New', monospace",
  "editor.fontSize": 14,
  "editor.lineHeight": 1.6,
  "editor.tabSize": 2,
  "editor.formatOnSave": true,
  "editor.minimap.enabled": false,
  "editor.bracketPairColorization.enabled": true,
  "editor.inlayHints.enabled": "on",
  "terminal.integrated.fontFamily": "'JetBrainsMono Nerd Font'",
  "terminal.integrated.fontSize": 13,
  "files.autoSave": "onFocusChange",
  "workbench.colorTheme": "Default Dark Modern",
  "explorer.confirmDelete": false,
  "[python]": { "editor.defaultFormatter": "ms-python.black-formatter" },
  "[go]": { "editor.defaultFormatter": "golang.go" },
  "[vue]": { "editor.defaultFormatter": "Vue.volar" }
}

扩展安装：

# 语言核心（必装）
code --install-extension golang.go
code --install-extension ms-python.python
code --install-extension ms-python.pylance
code --install-extension ms-python.black-formatter
code --install-extension Vue.volar

# 代码质量
code --install-extension esbenp.prettier-vscode
code --install-extension dbaeumer.vscode-eslint
code --install-extension streetsidesoftware.code-spell-checker

# Git
code --install-extension eamodio.gitlens
code --install-extension mhutchie.git-graph

# 容器与工具
code --install-extension ms-azuretools.vscode-docker
code --install-extension humao.rest-client
code --install-extension mechatroner.rainbow-csv
code --install-extension redhat.vscode-yaml

docs.title: Phase 5：Go 开发环境
docs.text:
为什么用官方 tarball： Ubuntu apt 仓库的 Go 版本通常落后官方 1–2 个版本。Go 的更新频率约每 6 个月一个大版本，工具链更新也依赖具体版本，建议直接用官方包。

# 获取最新版本号并安装
GO_LATEST=$(curl -s https://go.dev/VERSION?m=text | head -1)
echo "Installing: $GO_LATEST"

wget "https://go.dev/dl/${GO_LATEST}.linux-amd64.tar.gz" -O /tmp/go.tar.gz
sudo rm -rf /usr/local/go
sudo tar -C /usr/local -xzf /tmp/go.tar.gz
rm /tmp/go.tar.gz

source ~/.bashrc

验证： 

go version
go env GOPATH

安装核心 Go 工具：

go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest           # Language Server（VSCode Go 必需）
go install github.com/go-delve/delve/cmd/dlv@latest   # 调试器
go install golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest    # import 自动管理
go install github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/cmd/golangci-lint@latest  # Linter
go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest             # 热重载（开发期自动重启）

常见失败模式： - gopls: command not found：$GOPATH/bin 未在 PATH 中，检查 ~/.config/devenv/env.sh - go install 报网络超时：配置 GOPROXY go env -w GOPROXY=https://goproxy.cn,direct（国内环境）

docs.title: 6.1 安装 pyenv
docs.text:
为什么 pyenv 而不是 conda： conda 适合数据科学场景（大量二进制依赖），AI 应用开发场景（API 调用、服务开发）不需要 conda 的复杂性。pyenv 轻量、与 uv 配合更好。

sudo apt install -y \
  libssl-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev libreadline-dev \
  libsqlite3-dev libncursesw5-dev xz-utils tk-dev \
  libxml2-dev libxmlsec1-dev libffi-dev liblzma-dev

curl https://pyenv.run | bash

source ~/.bashrc

# 安装 Python 3.12（AI 生态支持最好的稳定版本）
pyenv install 3.12.0
pyenv global 3.12.0

python --version    # Python 3.12.0

docs.title: 6.2 安装 uv
docs.text:
为什么 uv 而不是 pip/poetry： uv 用 Rust 实现，依赖解析和安装速度比 pip 快 10–100 倍，兼容 pip 语法，内置 venv 管理，2024 年后已成为 Python 工具链新标准。

curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
source ~/.bashrc
uv --version

AI 项目典型依赖（在项目目录中按需安装）：

cd ~/projects/my-ai-app
uv init .
uv add anthropic                  # Claude SDK
uv add openai                     # OpenAI 兼容接口（Ollama 也用此）
uv add langchain langchain-anthropic langchain-community
uv add qdrant-client              # Qdrant 向量数据库
uv add sentence-transformers      # 本地 Embedding（CPU）
uv add fastapi "uvicorn[standard]"  # API 服务
uv add redis                      # Redis 客户端
uv add python-dotenv              # .env 文件加载
uv add pytest pytest-asyncio      # 测试

说明：sentence-transformers 在 CPU 环境下默认安装 CPU 版 PyTorch（约 500MB），首次 import 时自动下载 Embedding 模型（约 90MB）。

docs.title: Phase 7：Node.js / 前端环境
docs.text:
为什么 nvm 而不是 apt： apt 的 Node.js 通常是旧 LTS 版本。前端生态对 Node.js 版本敏感，nvm 支持多版本共存。

curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.40.1/install.sh | bash
source ~/.bashrc

nvm install --lts
nvm use --lts
nvm alias default lts/*

node --version && npm --version

安装 pnpm（替代 npm）：

为什么 pnpm： pnpm 通过硬链接共享依赖，磁盘占用比 npm/yarn 低 60%，安装速度更快，在 monorepo 场景下优势尤为明显。

npm install -g pnpm
pnpm --version

# Vue 项目脚手架
pnpm add -g @vue/cli
# 或使用 Vite（推荐）：pnpm create vite my-app --template vue-ts

docs.title: Phase 8：Docker 容器环境
docs.text:
# 清理旧版本
sudo apt remove -y docker docker-engine docker.io containerd runc 2>/dev/null || true

# 安装 Docker Engine
sudo install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings
curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg \
  | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg
sudo chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg

echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg] \
  https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu \
  $(lsb_release -cs) stable" \
  | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list

sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y \
  docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io \
  docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin

sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
sudo systemctl enable --now docker

注销并重新登录后验证： 

docker run hello-world
docker compose version

常见失败模式： - Got permission denied：未重新登录，newgrp docker 可临时生效 - lsb_release -cs 返回未知代号：手动查看 Docker 支持列表，Ubuntu 26.04 代号为 oracular 或 noble，确认后替换

docs.title: Phase 9：本地服务编排（docker-compose）
docs.text:
核心原则： 所有本地依赖服务通过一个 docker-compose.yml 统一管理，不手动 docker run。

mkdir -p ~/dev-services/data/{qdrant,postgres,redis,langfuse}
cd ~/dev-services

创建 ~/dev-services/docker-compose.yml：

# ~/dev-services/docker-compose.yml
# 本地开发服务编排：向量库 + 关系型DB + 缓存 + LLM可观测性
# 使用：docker compose up -d / docker compose down

services:

  # ── 向量数据库 ──────────────────────────────────────────
  qdrant:
    image: qdrant/qdrant:latest
    container_name: dev-qdrant
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "6333:6333"    # HTTP / REST API
      - "6334:6334"    # gRPC
    volumes:
      - ./data/qdrant:/qdrant/storage
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:6333/healthz"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3

  # ── PostgreSQL + pgvector ────────────────────────────────
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
    container_name: dev-postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: devuser
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <LOCAL_DEV_POSTGRES_PASSWORD>
      POSTGRES_DB: devdb
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - ./data/postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U devuser -d devdb"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # ── Redis ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: dev-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    volumes:
      - ./data/redis:/data
    command: redis-server --appendonly yes
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3

  # ── Langfuse（LLM 调用可观测性）────────────────────────
  langfuse:
    image: langfuse/langfuse:latest
    container_name: dev-langfuse
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://devuser:<LOCAL_DEV_POSTGRES_PASSWORD>@postgres:5432/langfuse
      NEXTAUTH_SECRET: <LOCAL_DEV_NEXTAUTH_SECRET>
      NEXTAUTH_URL: http://localhost:3000
      SALT: <LOCAL_DEV_SALT>

操作命令：

cd ~/dev-services

# 启动所有服务
docker compose up -d

# 查看状态
docker compose ps

# 查看日志
docker compose logs -f qdrant

# 停止（保留数据）
docker compose stop

# 停止并删除容器（数据保留在 ./data/）
docker compose down

验证所有服务：

# Qdrant
curl http://localhost:6333/healthz

# PostgreSQL
docker exec dev-postgres pg_isready -U devuser

# Redis
docker exec dev-redis redis-cli ping    # 应返回 PONG

# Langfuse Web UI：浏览器打开 http://localhost:3000
# 首次进入注册账号（本地账号，无需真实邮箱）

docs.title: 情况说明
docs.text:
Anthropic 官方不提供 Linux 版 Claude Desktop。官方为 Linux 开发者提供的路径是 Claude Code CLI（功能完整，支持 MCP）。

社区项目 aaddrick/claude-desktop-debian 通过重新打包官方 Windows Electron 包提供 Linux .deb 构建，是目前最成熟的非官方方案。两个选项适用于不同工作风格，按需选择，不需要都安装。

docs.title: 10.1 选项 A：Claude Code CLI（官方支持，推荐）
docs.text:
适合：终端工作流、AI 辅助编码、MCP 工具集成。

前置条件： Phase 7 已安装 Node.js。

npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
claude --version

# 认证（通过 API Key 或浏览器登录）
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="<ANTHROPIC_API_KEY>"
claude --print "Hello"    # 验证连通性

MCP 配置（连接本地工具）：

cat > ~/.claude.json << 'EOF'
{
  "mcpServers": {}
}
EOF

docs.title: 10.2 选项 B：非官方 Claude Desktop（社区维护）
docs.text:
适合：需要 GUI 界面、对话式工作流、不习惯纯终端。

⚠️ 声明： aaddrick/claude-desktop-debian 是社区项目，非 Anthropic 官方支持。 代码开源（MIT/Apache 双协议），使用 GPG 签名包，风险可控，但不等于官方安全背书。 安装前建议查阅项目 GitHub 页面确认最新状态。

# 安装前置依赖
sudo apt install -y libfuse2

# 添加 GPG Key 和仓库
curl -fsSL https://pkg.claude-desktop-debian.dev/KEY.gpg \
  | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/claude-desktop.gpg

echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/claude-desktop.gpg arch=amd64,arm64] \
  https://pkg.claude-desktop-debian.dev stable main" \
  | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/claude-desktop.list

sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y claude-desktop

GNOME Wayland 热键修复（如使用 Wayland 会话）： 

claude-desktop --install-gnome-hotkey

MCP 配置文件： ~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json

验证： 从应用菜单启动，登录 Anthropic 账号。

docs.title: Phase 11：Ollama（本地 LLM）
docs.text:
为什么： 开发期间反复调用 Claude API 有成本。Ollama 在本地运行开源小模型，提供 OpenAI 兼容接口。用本地模型验证业务流程，用 Claude API 做集成测试和生产调用。

CPU 环境的现实预期： 7B 模型在 8 核 CPU 上推理速度约 2–8 token/s（明显慢于 GPU），适合用于流程验证，不适合交互式使用。

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

# 验证安装
ollama --version

# 拉取 CPU 友好的小模型
# qwen2.5:1.5b - 约 1GB，CPU 速度较快，适合快速验证
ollama pull qwen2.5:1.5b

# 测试
ollama run qwen2.5:1.5b "Hello, respond with one sentence"

Ollama 服务管理： 

# Ollama 安装后自动注册 systemd 服务
sudo systemctl status ollama
sudo systemctl enable ollama    # 开机自启

# Ollama 暴露 OpenAI 兼容接口
curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags    # 列出已下载的模型

在代码中切换本地/云端模型（Python 示例）：

import os
from openai import OpenAI

# 开发期：本地 Ollama
if os.getenv("ENV") == "development":
    client = OpenAI(
        base_url="http://localhost:11434/v1",
        api_key="ollama"    # Ollama 不验证 key，随便填
    )
    model = "qwen2.5:1.5b"
else:
    # 生产：真实 API
    import anthropic
    # 使用 anthropic 客户端

docs.title: Phase 12：项目工作流模板
docs.text: 这部分不是安装步骤，而是每个新项目创建时的标准初始化模板。

docs.title: 12.1 Makefile 模板（AI 后端项目）
docs.text:
# Makefile - AI 后端项目工作流标准化
# 用法：make dev / make test / make lint / make clean

.PHONY: dev test lint clean build docker-up docker-down

# ── 开发 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dev: docker-up
    uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

dev-go:
    air    # Go 热重载（需安装 github.com/air-verse/air）

# ── 测试 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
test:
    uv run pytest tests/ -v --tb=short

test-unit:
    uv run pytest tests/unit/ -v

test-integration:
    uv run pytest tests/integration/ -v -m integration

# ── 代码质量 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
lint:
    uv run ruff check app/ tests/
    uv run mypy app/

lint-go:
    golangci-lint run ./...

format:
    uv run ruff format app/ tests/

# ── Docker 服务 ──────────────────────────────────────────────
docker-up:
    cd ~/dev-services && docker compose up -d
    @echo "等待服务就绪..."
    @sleep 3
    @docker exec dev-redis redis-cli ping > /dev/null && echo "✓ Redis"
    @curl -sf http://localhost:6333/healthz > /dev/null && echo "✓ Qdrant"

docker-down:
    cd ~/dev-services && docker compose stop

# ── 构建 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
build:
    uv build

# ── 清理 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
clean:
    find . -type d -name __pycache__ -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
    find . -name "*.pyc" -delete 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -rf .pytest_cache dist build

docs.title: 12.2 新项目初始化脚本
docs.text:
# ~/bin/new-ai-project（可执行，用于快速创建新项目）
cat > ~/.local/bin/new-ai-project << 'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -e

PROJECT_NAME="${1:?用法: new-ai-project <project-name>}"
PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/projects/$PROJECT_NAME"

mkdir -p "$PROJECT_DIR"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR"

# Git 初始化
git init
cp ~/.gitignore_global .gitignore

# direnv 配置
cat > .envrc << 'EOF'
# 项目环境变量 - 按需修改
export MODEL="claude-sonnet-4-6"
export LOG_LEVEL="debug"
EOF
direnv allow .

# Python 项目初始化
uv init .
uv add anthropic langchain langchain-anthropic qdrant-client fastapi "uvicorn[standard]" python-dotenv

# 复制 Makefile 模板
cp ~/templates/Makefile .

# 基础目录结构
mkdir -p app tests/{unit,integration} docs

echo "✓ 项目 $PROJECT_NAME 初始化完成: $PROJECT_DIR"
SCRIPT

chmod +x ~/.local/bin/new-ai-project
mkdir -p ~/projects ~/templates

docs.title: Phase 13：Langfuse 配置（LLM 可观测性）
docs.text:
为什么必须有可观测性： AI 应用的调试和优化依赖对每次 LLM 调用的追踪：prompt 内容、响应时间、token 消耗、检索命中率、向量相似度分布。没有这一层，debug 靠 print，优化靠猜。

Langfuse 已在 Phase 9 的 docker-compose 中包含，启动后访问 http://localhost:3000 注册本地账号。

Python 接入：

uv add langfuse

# 在项目入口处初始化
from langfuse import Langfuse
from langfuse.callback import CallbackHandler
import os

langfuse = Langfuse(
    public_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY"],
    host="http://localhost:3000"
)

# 与 LangChain 集成
langfuse_handler = CallbackHandler(
    public_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY"],
    host="http://localhost:3000"
)

# 使用
chain.invoke({"input": "..."}, config={"callbacks": [langfuse_handler]})

接入后可追踪： - 每次 LLM 调用的完整 prompt 和 response - Token 消耗（输入/输出分别统计） - 端到端 RAG pipeline 延迟分解 - 向量检索命中的 chunk 内容
